entry.title: Building ilo
entry.content:
ilo requires a certain set of software installed on your system in order to be built.

Prerequisites

  • git for version control
  • minisign to sign the waiver as a contributor
  • gpg to sign your the commit which adds the waiver

Manual Setup

  • Java JDK to compile the code
  • Maven to build the project
  • hugo in order to create the website
  • GraalVM to build a native executable

ilo Setup

  • ilo to open the reproducible build environment for ilo itself
  • One of the runtimes that ilo shell supports.

Building

In case you have Java and Maven locally installed call:

# run all tests
$ mvn verify

In case you have ilo installed, call this:

# build the project
$ ilo @build/once

# open a shell with a pre-defined build environment
$ ilo

In case you want to build the website do this:

# build website
$ hugo --minify --i18n-warnings --path-warnings --source docs

# serve website
$ hugo server --minify --i18n-warnings --path-warnings --source docs --watch

Take a look at the Makefile as an easy way to call all these commands.
